name: Cloud Solution Architecture Design Principles
description: Applying Google Cloud's architectural best practices including the Well-Architected Framework pillars to design reliable, secure, performant, and cost-optimized solutions.
key_facts: Google Cloud Architecture Framework pillars: Operational excellence, Security, Reliability, Cost optimization, Performance optimization
Design for failure: assume components will fail and architect for graceful degradation and automatic recovery
Loose coupling via Pub/Sub, Cloud Tasks, or event-driven patterns reduces blast radius of component failures
The four pillars of HA: Redundancy, Failover, Monitoring, and Testing (chaos engineering / game days)
Reference architectures for PCA case studies: EHR Healthcare (compliance, hybrid), HRL (streaming analytics), Mountkirk Games (global gaming), TerramEarth (IoT)
common_misconceptions: Highest availability always requires multi-region — multi-region adds significant cost and complexity; evaluate RTO/RPO requirements first
More services always means better architecture — over-engineering increases operational burden; use managed services to reduce complexity

name: SRE Practices and Reliability Design
description: Applying Site Reliability Engineering concepts — SLIs, SLOs, SLAs, error budgets — to define and maintain service reliability targets on Google Cloud.
key_facts: SLI (Service Level Indicator): specific quantitative measure of service level (e.g., request success rate, latency)
SLO (Service Level Objective): target value for an SLI (e.g., 99.9% requests succeed within 200ms)
SLA (Service Level Agreement): contractual commitment to SLO with financial penalties for violation
Error budget = 100% - SLO target; teams can use error budget for risky deployments; if exhausted, focus shifts to reliability
Toil is repetitive, manual operational work; SRE teams automate toil to keep it below 50% of engineering time
common_misconceptions: SLO of 100% is achievable and desirable — 100% SLOs are impossible and costly; 99.9-99.99% is realistic and allows innovation
SLA and SLO are interchangeable — SLAs are external contracts; SLOs are internal targets that should be stricter than SLAs

name: Network Architecture for Scalable Solutions
description: Designing VPC architectures including Shared VPC, VPC peering, multi-tier network designs, and hybrid connectivity patterns for enterprise workloads.
key_facts: Shared VPC allows a host project to share subnets with service projects; all resources share one centralized VPC
VPC peering connects VPCs with non-overlapping CIDRs; routes are not transitive
Hub-and-spoke topology using Shared VPC or Network Connectivity Center enables centralized egress and inspection
Cloud Interconnect (Dedicated or Partner) provides SLA-backed private connectivity from on-premises to GCP
Private Google Access enables VMs without external IPs to reach Google APIs; Private Service Connect modernizes this with VPC endpoints
common_misconceptions: You can have overlapping IP ranges in peered VPCs — peered VPCs must have non-overlapping primary and secondary ranges
Cloud VPN and Cloud Interconnect offer the same SLA — VPN is best-effort over the internet; Interconnect offers 99.99% SLA

name: Security Architecture and Defense in Depth
description: Designing layered security across identity, network, data, and application tiers using Google Cloud security services and zero-trust principles.
key_facts: Defense in depth: layer security controls at identity, network perimeter, application, and data levels
BeyondCorp Enterprise implements zero-trust access — verify identity and device posture before granting application access
VPC Service Controls create security perimeters around sensitive GCP resources to prevent data exfiltration
Cloud Armor provides WAF and DDoS protection for external load balancers with OWASP rule sets
Security Command Center provides unified threat detection, vulnerability scanning, and compliance monitoring
common_misconceptions: A VPN connection to GCP means the network is fully secure — network security is just one layer; IAM, data encryption, and app security are equally critical
VPC Service Controls replace IAM — they work together; VPC SC adds perimeter controls while IAM controls identity-based access

name: Selecting the Right Storage Service
description: Matching workload characteristics (consistency, latency, throughput, query patterns) to the optimal GCP storage service across relational, NoSQL, and analytical options.
key_facts: Cloud Spanner: globally distributed relational DB, strong consistency, unlimited scale, use for financial/inventory systems
Cloud SQL: managed MySQL/PostgreSQL/SQL Server, regional HA, best for existing relational workloads up to a few TB
Bigtable: wide-column NoSQL, single-digit ms latency, best for time-series, IoT, and high-throughput reads/writes
Firestore: serverless document DB with real-time sync, best for mobile/web apps needing offline support
BigQuery: analytical warehouse for OLAP, not suitable for OLTP; use federated queries to join with operational DBs
common_misconceptions: Cloud Spanner is just a scaled-up Cloud SQL — Spanner uses a completely different architecture (TrueTime, Paxos) enabling global consistency
Bigtable supports SQL queries — Bigtable uses a key-value model with no SQL support; use BigQuery for SQL analytics on Bigtable data

name: Cost Optimization Strategies
description: Identifying and implementing cost optimization techniques across compute, storage, and networking to reduce cloud spend while maintaining performance.
key_facts: Right-sizing VMs using Cloud Monitoring recommender reduces over-provisioned compute costs
Preemptible/Spot VMs reduce batch compute costs by 60-91% for fault-tolerant workloads
Object Lifecycle Management automatically transitions data to cheaper storage classes based on age
BigQuery slot reservations reduce query costs for consistent high-volume workloads vs on-demand pricing
Committed Use Discounts (1 or 3 year) offer 37-57% savings on Compute Engine vCPUs and memory
common_misconceptions: Deleting unused projects immediately stops all billing — some resources like load balancers have static charges; verify all resources are deleted
Smaller machine types always cost less — if you need more instances for the same work, the total cost may be higher

name: Cloud Migration Planning and Execution
description: Planning and executing large-scale migrations to Google Cloud using the 4-phase migration framework: assess, plan, deploy, and optimize.
key_facts: Migration phases: Assess (inventory, dependencies) > Plan (migration strategy, architecture) > Deploy (pilot, migrate) > Optimize
Migrate to Virtual Machines (formerly Velostrata) enables live VM migration from VMware/Hyper-V/AWS to Compute Engine
Database Migration Service (DMS) handles homogeneous and heterogeneous database migrations with minimal downtime
Strangler Fig pattern gradually replaces monolith functionality with microservices to reduce migration risk
Transfer Appliance (offline) and Storage Transfer Service (online) move large datasets to Cloud Storage
common_misconceptions: You must migrate everything at once — phased migration with pilot workloads reduces risk and validates architecture decisions
Cloud migration is purely technical — organizational change management, training, and governance are equally critical to success

name: High Availability and Disaster Recovery Patterns
description: Designing HA architectures with multi-zone and multi-region deployments, defining RPO/RTO, and selecting appropriate DR patterns (warm standby, pilot light, hot standby).
key_facts: RPO (Recovery Point Objective): maximum acceptable data loss measured in time; RTO (Recovery Time Objective): maximum acceptable downtime
Multi-zone deployments protect against zone failures; multi-region protects against region failures (rare but possible)
Cloud Spanner offers 99.999% SLA in multi-region configurations; Cloud SQL HA is 99.95% within a region
Global load balancing with health checks automatically reroutes traffic to healthy regions during a regional outage
DR patterns by cost/recovery time: Backup-and-restore (cheapest, slowest) < Pilot light < Warm standby < Active-active (expensive, fastest)
common_misconceptions: Multi-zone equals multi-region HA — zone failures are common (hardware); region failures are rare; assess if multi-region cost is justified
Cloud Spanner replaces all HA architecture needs — you still need application-level retry logic and global load balancing

name: Microservices and Event-Driven Architecture
description: Designing microservices-based applications on GCP using containers, service meshes, and event-driven patterns with Pub/Sub and Cloud Tasks.
key_facts: Microservices decompose monoliths into independently deployable, scalable services with bounded contexts
Pub/Sub decouples services with asynchronous messaging; supports push (HTTP) and pull delivery models
Cloud Tasks manages distributed task execution with guaranteed delivery and configurable rate limiting
Cloud Service Mesh (Anthos Service Mesh / Istio) provides traffic management, mTLS security, and observability for microservices
API Gateway and Apigee manage API lifecycle, authentication, rate limiting, and monetization for microservices
common_misconceptions: Microservices always outperform monoliths — microservices add network latency and operational complexity; monoliths can outperform at small scale
Pub/Sub guarantees exactly-once delivery — Pub/Sub guarantees at-least-once delivery; consumers must implement idempotency

name: Compliance and Regulatory Frameworks on GCP
description: Implementing compliance controls for HIPAA, PCI DSS, GDPR, and other frameworks using Google Cloud's compliance offerings and configuration tools.
key_facts: Google Cloud has BAAs for HIPAA; customers must configure services to comply (e.g., enable audit logging, use approved services)
CMEK (Customer-Managed Encryption Keys) meets data sovereignty requirements by keeping key control with the customer
VPC Service Controls restrict sensitive data movement to approved perimeters to prevent data exfiltration
Assured Workloads enforces compliance controls (FedRAMP, IL2/IL4, ITAR) at the project level automatically
Cloud Audit Logs provide data access, admin activity, and system event logs for compliance and forensics
common_misconceptions: Using Google Cloud means you are automatically HIPAA compliant — compliance requires customer-side configuration and a signed BAA
Compliance frameworks are the same globally — GDPR (EU data residency), HIPAA (US healthcare), and PCI DSS (payment) have different requirements

name: DevOps and CI/CD Pipeline Architecture
description: Designing CI/CD pipelines using Cloud Build, Cloud Deploy, and Artifact Registry to automate testing and deployment of applications to GKE and Cloud Run.
key_facts: Cloud Build triggers builds from Cloud Source Repositories, GitHub, or Bitbucket on push or PR events
Artifact Registry stores and manages container images, Maven, npm, and Python packages with IAM integration
Cloud Deploy manages delivery pipelines with promotion sequences, approval gates, and rollback capabilities
Binary Authorization enforces a policy requiring signed container images before deployment to GKE or Cloud Run
SLSA (Supply-chain Levels for Software Artifacts) framework defines levels of software supply chain security
common_misconceptions: Cloud Build and Cloud Deploy do the same thing — Build creates artifacts; Deploy manages release pipelines with promotion and rollback
Container signing is just a best practice — Binary Authorization enforces it as a hard requirement, preventing unsigned image deployment

name: Analytics Architecture and Lakehouse Design
description: Architecting modern data analytics platforms using BigQuery, Dataflow, and Dataplex to support both batch and streaming data processing pipelines.
key_facts: Lambda architecture has separate batch and speed layers; Kappa architecture uses a single streaming layer for both
Dataflow (Apache Beam) executes the same code for batch and streaming, simplifying the Kappa architecture on GCP
BigQuery as a lakehouse: external tables query data directly in Cloud Storage; partitioning and clustering optimize performance
Dataplex provides data governance, data quality, and data discovery across distributed data assets
Event-driven analytics: Pub/Sub -> Dataflow -> BigQuery pipeline enables real-time streaming analytics
common_misconceptions: A data lake replaces a data warehouse — a lakehouse combines both: raw data storage with warehouse query performance
BigQuery is only for batch analytics — BigQuery supports streaming inserts and near-real-time queries

name: Managed vs Self-Managed Services Decision Framework
description: Evaluating when to use fully managed GCP services versus self-managed infrastructure based on operational overhead, customization needs, and cost.
key_facts: Managed services reduce operational burden but may limit customization (e.g., Cloud SQL vs self-managed PostgreSQL on VM)
For Kafka, self-hosted on GKE offers full control; Pub/Sub is fully managed with different semantics
GKE Autopilot vs Standard: Autopilot removes node management overhead but restricts some advanced configurations
Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) for managed services includes service fees but excludes operational personnel costs
Open-source compatibility: Cloud Bigtable is HBase-compatible; Dataproc is managed Hadoop/Spark
common_misconceptions: Managed services are always more expensive than self-managed — when accounting for operational staff time, managed services often cost less overall
Self-managed gives total control — cloud providers still manage underlying hardware, network fabric, and physical security

name: Hybrid and Multi-Cloud Architecture with Anthos
description: Designing hybrid cloud solutions using Anthos to run consistent Kubernetes workloads across on-premises data centers, GCP, AWS, and Azure.
key_facts: Anthos enables running GKE clusters on-premises (Anthos clusters on VMware/bare metal) and on other clouds
Config Sync applies Kubernetes configurations from a Git repository consistently across all Anthos clusters
Anthos Service Mesh (based on Istio) provides unified traffic management, observability, and security across clusters
Cloud Interconnect and Cloud VPN connect on-premises to GCP; choosing depends on bandwidth and SLA requirements
Multi-cluster Ingress routes global traffic to the nearest healthy Anthos cluster using Google's global load balancer
common_misconceptions: Anthos only works on GCP — Anthos is specifically designed to run on-premises and on other cloud providers (AWS, Azure)
Hybrid cloud and multi-cloud are the same — hybrid connects on-premises with cloud; multi-cloud uses multiple cloud providers

name: Observability and Cloud Operations Suite
description: Designing observability strategies using Cloud Monitoring, Cloud Logging, Cloud Trace, and Cloud Profiler to achieve full-stack visibility for production systems.
key_facts: The three pillars of observability: Metrics (Cloud Monitoring), Logs (Cloud Logging), and Traces (Cloud Trace)
Custom metrics enable application-level monitoring beyond infrastructure metrics using Cloud Monitoring API or OpenTelemetry
Log-based metrics convert log entries into metrics for alerting on patterns like error rates or specific log messages
Cloud Profiler continuously profiles CPU and memory usage in production with minimal overhead (less than 1%)
Error Reporting automatically aggregates and tracks unhandled exceptions from Cloud Logging entries across services
common_misconceptions: Cloud Monitoring only monitors GCP resources — it also supports AWS resources, on-premises hosts, and custom metrics from any source
Logging everything is always better — high-volume logging increases cost and noise; use log severity levels and sampling for high-throughput services
